markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has confirmed Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 console launch on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S after multiple delays aimed at extra polish, with Take-Two Interactive's recent earnings explicitly tying record revenue forecasts to that date. This extended timeline through late 2026 creates a wide window for high-profile events in gaming, tech, music, and global affairs, shaping trader focus on outcomes like major AI model releases or artist projects. Upcoming catalysts include Take-Two's next earnings call and potential additional trailers, while the delay has already shifted fall 2026 release schedules across the industry. The market reflects skin-in-the-game consensus on what verifiable developments could realistically precede the game's arrival.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials and stalled retrials in New York, including the May 2026 deadlocked jury on the Jessica Mann allegations, combined with Weinstein’s ongoing appeal of his 16-year Los Angeles conviction, have driven trader consensus toward “No Prison Time” as the dominant outcome. The 74-year-old former Miramax executive has already served years in custody amid repeated procedural reversals and mixed verdicts, while health concerns and potential time-served credits further limit prospects for substantial additional sentencing. Upcoming hearings on whether prosecutors will pursue another Mann retrial and California appellate rulings remain key catalysts that could shift implied probabilities if new convictions materialize.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights' road victory in a back-and-forth Game 1 of the 2026 Stanley Cup Final has flipped trader consensus, positioning them as the slight favorite over the Carolina Hurricanes. Carolina entered the series with one of the strongest playoff runs in recent memory, boasting superior possession metrics and expected goals dominance after topping the Eastern Conference with 113 regular-season points. Vegas countered with efficient high-danger scoring and momentum from a dominant Western Conference Final sweep, overcoming Carolina's historical edge in similar matchups. The narrow implied probabilities reflect the closely contested nature of the remaining games, where home-ice advantage, goaltending consistency, and special-teams execution could still shift outcomes in either direction.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: MegaETH airdrop by...?
events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities among France, Spain, and England reflects deep European parity heading into the expanded 48-team tournament, with each side boasting elite depth, strong recent qualifying campaigns, and favorable group draws. Spain maintains consistency through its possession-oriented style and attacking talent despite minor injury concerns, while France leverages squad versatility and defensive structure. England’s momentum stems from clean qualifying results and tactical adjustments under its current manager. Argentina and Brazil remain competitive via established cores and CONMEBOL pedigree, but the top trio’s edge in current form, home-continent familiarity, and historical knockout success keeps their shares narrowly ahead in trader consensus.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of implied probabilities among France, Spain, and England reflects deep European parity heading into the expanded 48-team tournament, with each side boasting elite depth, strong recent qualifying campaigns, and favorable group draws. markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping continues to exercise unchallenged authority over the Chinese Communist Party’s leadership institutions, with recent personnel appointments, military reshuffles, and anti-corruption measures reinforcing his position rather than signaling transition. Official activities in 2025 and early 2026, including directives on the 15th Five-Year Plan for 2026–2030 and emphasis on party self-governance, reflect sustained focus on continuity ahead of the 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027. No verified institutional challenges, factional disputes, or public statements indicate removal from the roles of general secretary, president, or Central Military Commission chairman before the end of 2026. Trader consensus at 93.2% for “No” aligns with this absence of disruptive developments, though late-breaking events such as health issues or major internal crises could still alter outcomes.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: The wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field shows Gavin Newsom leading trader consensus at 25.1 percent, ahead of a fragmented group including Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris. This positioning stems from Newsom’s established national profile as California governor, prior campaign infrastructure, and visibility in swing-state outreach, while no single alternative has consolidated early endorsements or polling momentum. The remaining probability is dispersed across sitting senators, governors, and outside voices, reflecting post-2024 uncertainty, the absence of an incumbent advantage, and varying paths through primaries that reward fundraising, name recognition, and appeal to key voting blocs. Scheduled events such as the 2026 midterms and subsequent primary calendar could shift support as candidates test viability in early states.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field shows Gavin Newsom leading trader consensus at 25.1 percent, ahead of a fragmented group including Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris. This positioning stems from Newsom’s established national profile as California governor, prior campaign infrastructure, and visibility in swing-state outreach, while no single alternative has consolidated early endorsements or polling momentum. The remaining probability is dispersed across sitting senators, governors, and outside voices, reflecting post-2024 uncertainty, the absence of an incumbent advantage, and varying paths through primaries that reward fundraising, name recognition, and appeal to key voting blocs. Scheduled events such as the 2026 midterms and subsequent primary calendar could shift support as candidates test viability in early states.
